response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software developer with strong web, backend, and software craftsmanship credentials, but the resume shows no evidence of the core requirements for a Principal Performance Architect role: SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator or GPU architecture experience, deep-learning workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design. The closest relevant experience is low-latency DLang services and some C/C++ tooling work, which falls well short of the hardware performance architecture depth this principal-level role demands.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Broad technical range across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with 15+ years of experience meeting the BS+8 bar
Strong engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD) and open-source/community contributions
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon, or hardware architecture experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or accelerator background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not performance architecture at the hardware-software boundary

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN workload background
Systems-level performance work (low-latency DLang, async I/O)
